[File: 'src/BNBTransferScript.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain'] [Function: transferBNB] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER who is any external caller invoke transferBNB(attacker, X) on a deployed BorrowerWrappersScript instance that holds BNB (from a prior claimSPRewardsAndRecycle or claimStakingGainsAndRecycle execution that left residual BNB), with no preconditions other than the script holding a nonzero ETH balance, by calling BorrowerWrappersScript.transferBNB(attacker, address(this).balance) directly, violating the invariant that BNB held by the script belongs exclusively to the script owner and must only be forwarded through authorized protocol flows, causing scoped impact: attacker drains all BNB held by the script contract without any protocol authorization?
